name: Angus Anywhere
description: A tenant-request and operations platform commonly used in commercial property to log, route, and monitor work orders and building service requests.
whyUseIt: Angus-style systems bring discipline to service delivery. They give teams one queue for tenant requests, work-order routing, response tracking, and documentation, which is critical when service quality and communication speed shape tenant retention.
aiCapabilities: AI can support triage by classifying work requests, suggesting urgency levels, and summarizing recurring complaint patterns.
workflowStage: Tenant request intake and work-order management
propertyManagerWorkflow: Property managers monitor open requests, escalate critical issues, follow contractor attendance, and make sure tenants receive updates before frustration builds.
managerExpects: Fast acknowledgment, clean categorization, closed-loop communication, and no blind spots on overdue or repeatedly reopened jobs.
aiWorkflowImpact: AI can shorten queue review by identifying high-risk requests, duplicate complaints, and assets with recurring failure patterns.
demoDescription: Review a day's open request queue, sort it into urgent, today, and monitor categories, then draft the next update for two waiting tenants.

vocabulary.definition: A tracked instruction for maintenance or service work tied to a building issue or tenant request.
Service level expectation for response or resolution time.
How often a supposedly resolved issue returns, signaling poor fix quality or weak diagnosis.

beginnerMistakes: Closing jobs before the tenant has actually confirmed resolution
Routing requests with the wrong urgency code
Using the system as a ticket dump instead of a service-management tool
